current_suggestion.pitch: Renaissance Periodization gives you the programming science side, but Jeff Nippard steelmans the 'naturalistic' training perspective — he emphasizes practical hypertrophy application with heavy citations of primary literature, often reaching different conclusions than RP on exercise selection, frequency, and volume landmarks. His content is solo or interview-style, typically 10–25 minutes, and he regularly covers recovery, injury prevention, and the gap between what research says versus what actually works in the gym. He fills a niche RP doesn't: translating meta-analyses into specific, executable training decisions for intermediate-to-advanced lifters.

history: TEDx Talks – Russ Roberts (EconTalk host solo appearances)
EP Wealth Advisors (with Ben Felix-style rigor — try 'The Plain Bagel' instead)
